mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates broad-based economic strength and improving corporate revenue prospects, leading to positive equity returns as investors price in higher future earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has shown consistent growth since the 2009 trough, signaling recovery.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between PAYEMS 3-month momentum and 20-day forward returns is less than 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation (CPIAUCSL) breaches a 2.5% threshold, it forces the central bank to consider monetary tightening. The market anticipates higher discount rates, compressing equity valuations and causing negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical Fed tightening cycles are often preceded by rising inflation.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and a decrease in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a severe economic downturn where both labor and capital are underutilized. This joint signal of weakness leads to sharp downward revisions in earnings and negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:These two indicators moved in concert during the 2008-2009 recession.
failure_condition: The combined signal does not produce a mean forward return that is statistically significantly lower (t-test p > 0.05) than the mean return from either individual trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate policy (ZIRP) regime, strong growth in housing starts (HOUST) signals robust consumer confidence and credit creation. This effect, amplified by low financing costs (FEDFUNDS), drives broad economic activity and positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The current ZIRP environment has been in place since 2008.
failure_condition: The correlation between HOUST momentum and S&P 500 forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates the supply side of the economy is expanding robustly, often preceding strong corporate earnings reports. This positive signal on productive capacity leads to positive equity market returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is a key component of the Index of Coincident Economic Indicators.
failure_condition: Instances of triggered INDPRO acceleration are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns more than 40% of the time over the full backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: A surprisingly strong monthly increase in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates higher-than-expected consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues. The market reacts quickly to this positive demand signal, driving positive short-term returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending constitutes approximately two-thirds of US GDP.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following a trigger event is not statistically different from the average return on non-trigger days (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is near zero and inflation (CPIAUCSL) begins to accelerate, it signals an impending shift away from accommodative monetary policy. The market prices in future rate hikes, leading to higher discount rates and negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Market sensitivity to 'taper tantrum' discussions in 2013.
failure_condition: During triggered periods, the S&P 500 has positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, indicating the market is not pricing in tightening.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in real GDP growth (GDPC1) below a key threshold is the most direct signal of a slowing economy. This implies lower aggregate corporate earnings growth, leading to negative revisions in equity valuations and negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 is the broadest measure of economic output.
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate (correctly predicting negative returns when triggered) is below 55% over any 5-year rolling window.
